info.title: ID Service
info.description:
Manage Authentication, Clients, User, Profile and Permissions Settings

 **Last update**: 18 feb 26, 12:38 p. m.

tags.name: API Key
tags.description:
API Keys allow you to authenticate your applications and services to the API with static credentials.

Use this endpoints to centrally manage API Keys for all users. For self-service management, use the [User API Keys](/v2/service/id#tag/User-ApiKey) endpoints.

tags.name: Access Policy
tags.description:
Access Policies allow you to enforce security measures and best practices.

🛡️ **Security Hub Enterprise** is required to create multiple Access Policies.

tags.name: Domain
tags.description:
Trusted domains allow you to create users without the need of validating their email addresses.
Users still must be [invited](#operation/inviteUser), but their invitation will be automatically approved.

After you create your domain, you will need to ask our Support team to approve it before you can use it.
Please create a Support ticket with a link to the created domain.

tags.name: User ApiKey
tags.description:
For self-service management of API Keys.

Use this endpoints for self-service management of API Keys. For centrally managing API Keys for all users, use the [API Key](/v2/service/id#tag/API-Key) endpoints.

emailSearch.description: The user's email (Allows partial search, for example searching for all users with a given email domain such as `@mydomain.com`)

email.description: The user's email (**Full email only**. No partial search. Case sensitive.)

search.description: This filter will apply to the `firstname`, `lastname`, `email` and `documentNumber` fields

effectiveAccessWarehouseId.description: The Warehouse ID that the user has access to. This refer to the `id` of the **Warehouse** Entity. See [Warehouse](/v2/service/wms#tag/Warehouse).

PreAuthorizeOauth.description: The request was correct and the App exists. It will return a redirectUrl if no further authorization is needed or the app information if user needs to authorize it

AuthorizeOauth.description: The request was correct and the App is authorized. User should be redirected with the requested grants in the query string parameters

token_type.description: The type of the access token.

expires_in.description: The expiration time of the access token in seconds

access_token.description: The access token to consume Janis API

id_token.description: The OpenID Connect token that includes user data. Only present if openid scope was authorized. It may include more information if the profile and/or email scopes were authorized too

refresh_token.description: A long lived token used to mantain a user session for a longer period. Only present if offline_access scope was authorized.

PublicIdentityProviderList.description: The providers were fetched with no errors

schema.description: A public list of Identity Providers

AuthorizeIdentityProvider.description: The request was correct and the Provider is configured. User should be redirected to the Identity Provider Authorization page.

AuthorizeCallbackIdentityProvider.description: The user successfully authorized and logged in. User should be redirected to the redirect URL.

id.description: The unique identifier of the item

dateModified.description: The date of the last modification

message.description: When user is locked after failed attempts

invalidAuth.description: When the auth is invalid or no generated by JanisID

message.description: A message with invalid auth reason
A message explaining token is expired

LoggedUserTokens.description: The tokens for an authenticated user

token_type.description: The type of the access token.

expires_in.description: The expiration time of the access token in seconds

scope.description: The authorized scope separated by a space character

access_token.description: The access token to consume Janis API

id_token.description: The OpenID Connect token that includes user data.

UploadData.description: The data required to upload data to Amazon Bucket

Content-Type.description: The content type to request Amazon

key.description: The request's key to put the data

profileIds.description: The list of Profile IDs to apply the policy to

allowedMechanisms.description: The list of allowed authentication mechanisms
The list of allowed MFA mechanisms

maxExpirationDays.description:
The maximum expiration days. If set as `null` or `0`, the API key expiration is not enforced

🛡️ **Security Hub Enterprise** is required to enforce API Key and max expiration time.

AccessPolicyPriority.description:
The priority of the Access Policy. This is used to decide which policy should be applied in case more than one targets a given user.

The policy with the lowest priority will be applied.

description.example: The description of the Access Policy

ApiKeyUsageRecord.description: A data point of API Key usage

from.description: The start of the date range as ISOString

to.description: The end of the date range as ISOString

totalRequests.description: The total number of requests made by the API Key during the date range

successfulRequests.description: The number of successful requests made by the API Key during the date range

failedRequests.description: The number of failed requests made by the API Key during the date range

errorRate.description: The error rate of the API Key during the date range as a rounded percentage

ApiKeyHealth.description: The health of an API Key. If any of the metrics reach the critical threshold, the API Key may be blocked.

volume.description: The volume of requests made by the API Key in the health check period

max.description: The maximum number of requests made by the API Key in one hour during the health check period

sum.description: The sum of requests made by the API Key in the health check period

sampleCount.description: The number of hours with requests made by the API Key during the health check period

avg.description: The average number of requests made by the API Key per hour during the health check period. This will only account for hours with requests.

score.description: The score of the API Key's volume

x-enumDescriptions.low: The API Key is operating normally with a low volume of requests
x-enumDescriptions.medium: The API Key is experiencing moderate load with a moderate volume of requests
x-enumDescriptions.high: The API Key is experiencing high load with a high volume of requests
x-enumDescriptions.critical: The API Key is experiencing critical load with a very high volume of requests. This can be considered abuse or a potential DoS attack.

errorRate.description: The error rate of the API Key during the health check period as a rounded percentage

totalRequests.description: The total number of requests made by the API Key during the health check period

failedRequests.description: The number of failed requests made by the API Key during the health check period

percentage.description: The error rate of the API Key during the health check period as a rounded percentage

score.description: The score of the API Key's error rate

x-enumDescriptions.optimal: The API Key is operating normally with zero error rate
x-enumDescriptions.low: The API Key is experiencing a low error rate
x-enumDescriptions.medium: The API Key is experiencing a moderate error rate
x-enumDescriptions.high: The API Key is experiencing a high error rate
x-enumDescriptions.critical: The API Key is experiencing a very high error rate. This can be considered an issue with the API Key.

expireAfter.description:
The number of days until the API Key expires. This cannot be greater than the [Access Policy](/v2/service/id#tag/Access-Policy)'s max expiration days.
When set, the API Key will have a `validUntil` date set. This field will not be returned when reading the API Key.

The owner of the API Key will receive a notification before expiration to generate a new one.

🛡️ **Security Hub Enterprise** is required to enforce API Key expiration and max expiration time via Access Policies.

validUntil.description:
The API Key's expiration date. After this date, the API Key will no longer be valid.

The owner of the API Key will receive a notification before expiration to generate a new one.

🛡️ **Security Hub Enterprise** is required to enforce API Key expiration and max expiration time via Access Policies.

revokedBy.description: The ID of the user who revoked the API Key. This will be set when the API Key is revoked manually and the status is set to 'revoked'.

revocationDate.description: The date when the API Key was revoked. This will be set when the API Key is revoked manually and the status is set to 'revoked'.

x-enumDescriptions.active: The API Key is active and can be used
x-enumDescriptions.revoked: The API Key has been revoked manually (see `revokedBy` and `revocationDate` to get information about the revocation)
x-enumDescriptions.expired: The API Key has expired due to expiration date (the `validUntil` date has been reached)

usage.description: The usage metrics of the API Key (this will be automatically updated and will reflect the last 24 hours of usage)

age.description: The age of the API Key in days (since creation date)

lastActivity.description:
The date of the last activity of the API Key

🛡️ **Security Hub Enterprise** is required to see the last activity date.

inactivityPeriod.description: The inactivity period of the API Key in days (since last activity)

clientCode.description: The user's actual client code. See [Client](/v2/service/id#tag/Client).

useMfaMechanism.description: Indicates if the user is using MFA mechanism for the current client

requiredByPolicy.description: Indicates if the user is required to use MFA mechanism for the current client by the access policy

PublicAppData.description: The public information of an APP

description.description: A short description about the App
description.example: The best App for doing something

privacyPolicyUrl.description: The URL of the APP privacy policy

logoUrl.description: The URL of the APP logo

type.description: The type of APP Client. This determines the available OAuth flows and requirements. This cannot be updated once the APP has been created.

clientSecret.description: The OAuth Client Secret. Only for confidential APPs.

redirectUris.description: The list of allowed redirect URIs

isInternal.description: Indicates if the APP is an internal Janis APP

EmailTrustedDomainResult.description: The result of the domain name check

trusted.description: Whether the email has a trusted domain

tiers.description: The current client tiers for each product

securityHub.description: The current client tier for the Security Hub product

x-enumDescriptions.standard: Standard access to Security Hub features (self-enforced policies)
x-enumDescriptions.enterprise: Enterprise access to Security Hub features (admin-enforced policies, automation-focused)

clientCode.description: The code of the client that owns the provider. null if the provider is global.

title.description: The human friendly name of the provider

name.description: A unique URL-friendly name of the Provider. This must be unique across all Janis providers. It must be lower case.

code.description: The internal code that will be used to avoid offering multiple providers for the same source

referenceIdField.description:
The name of the field that will contain the user referenceId used for identity matching. This is only valid for tenant-specific providers.
If this is empty, emails will be matched instead.

logoUrl.description: The URL to the Identity Provider's logo

authorizeUrl.description: The URL were the user will be redirected to authorize Janis

tokenUrl.description: The URL that Janis will use to retrieve the ID Token

userInfoUrl.description: The URL that Janis will use to retrieve the User Info

jwksUrl.description: The URL where JWKS is published for ID Tokens validation

clientId.description: The OAuth client ID registered in the provider authorization server

clientSecret.description: The OAuth client Secret registered in the provider authorization server

clientCertificate.description: The OAuth client certificate registered in the provider authorization server in PEM Format

privateKey.description: The OAuth Private Key registered in the provider authorization server in PEM Format

firstname.description: The user's firstname. If User exists and has firstname, not going to change.

lastname.description: The user's lastname. If User exists and has lastname, not going to change.

temporalPassword.description:
A temporal password to set to the user. This password will be changed on first login.
**IMPORTANT**:This password will only be honored when inviting a non-external user with a [Trusted domain](#tag/Domain). For existing users or users on non-trusted domains, the password will be ignored.

message.description: When email or password are incorrect

nextStep.description: The Step to continue with the login

name.description: The name of the authentication mechanism data

requireNumbers.description: Numbers are required in the password

disallowPersonalData.description: The password cannot contain any words of personal data, e.g. name, e-mail address, etc.

disallowConsecutiveNumbers.description: The password cannot contain 3 or more consecutive numbers

key.description:
The permission key. This key is made with 3 the properties `service`, `entity` and
`action` separated by a colon.

pickingPick.description: Allows to users to use Pick module from the Janis Order APP

pickingControl.description: Allows to users to use Control module from the Janis Order APP

deliveryShippingContainer.description: Allows to users to use Shipping Container module from the Janis Delivery APP

wmsOrderMovement.description: Allows to users to use OrderMovement from the Janis Inventory APP

wmsPackagePositioning.description: Allows to users to use PackagePositioning from the Janis Inventory APP

wmsSupplying.description: Allows to users to use Supplying from the Janis Inventory APP

wmsProductMovement.description: Allows to users to use ProductMovement from the Janis Inventory APP

wmsStockControl.description: Allows to users to use Stock Control from the Janis Inventory APP

wmsOrderReception.description: Allows to users to use Order Reception from the Janis Inventory APP

disallowPasswordLogin.description: Enable this to prevent users from logging in using their passwords. If this field is not defined or `clientDefault` option is selected, this profile use client config by default. This is useful when an Identity Provider must be used, for example Active Directory. *WARNING! Be sure you have an identity provider properly configured before setting this to true. Otherwise, you will get locked out.*

automaticallyDeactivateInactiveAccounts.description:
Automatically deactivate accounts without any activity in the last 90 days

🛡️ **Security Hub Enterprise** is required to automatically deactivate accounts without any activity in the last 90 days.

ValidatePermission.description: An api permission endpoint and/or permission key

ValidatePermissionResult.description: An api permission endpoint and/or permission key with the results

ValidatePermissions.description: An array of api permissions endpoints and/or permission keys

ValidatePermissionsResults.description: An array of api permissions endpoints and/or permission keys with the ValidatePermissionsResults

AuthorizedUserList.description: The list of authorized user requests

userId.description: ID of the dev user requesting authorization

requestedHours.description: Number of hours requested for authorization (mutually exclusive with requestedDays)

requestedDays.description: Number of days requested for authorization (mutually exclusive with requestedHours)

authorizedFrom.description: Date when authorization starts (only set when approved)

authorizedTo.description: Date when authorization ends (only set when approved)

approvedBy.description: ID of the user who approved the authorization

rejectedBy.description: ID of the user who rejected the authorization

cancelledBy.description: ID of the user who cancelled the authorization

revokedBy.description: ID of the user who revoked the authorization

status.description: The status at this history entry

comment.description: Optional comment for the status change
comment.example: Need access to debug production issue

userId.description: ID of the user who made the status change

country.description:
The client's country's ISO code in three letters.
This field refer to the `iso3` of the **country** Entity from [I18N service](/v2/service/i18N#tag/Country).

language.description:
The client's preferred language.
This field refer to the `fullCode` of the **Language** Entity. See [Language](/v2/service/i18n#tag/Languages).

timezone.description:
The client's timezone.
This field refer to the `name` of the **timezone** Entity from [I18N service](/v2/service/i18N#tag/Timezone).

currency.description:
The client's currency.
This field refer to the `code` of the **currency** Entity from [I18N service](/v2/service/i18N#tag/Currency).

currencySymbol.description:
The client's currency symbol.
This field refer to the `symbol` of the **currency** Entity from [I18N service](/v2/service/i18N#tag/Currency).

currencyDisplay.description: Determines which Currency field should be displayed in Janis, the symbol or the code.

organizationId.description:
The Organization ID.
This field refer to the `id` of the **Organization** Entity from [ID Service](/v2/service/id#tag/Organization).

disallowPasswordLogin.description: Enable this to prevent users from logging in using their passwords. This is useful when an Identity Provider must be used, for example Active Directory. *WARNING! Be sure you have an identity provider properly configured before setting this to true. Otherwise, you will get locked out.*

email.description: The security team's email. Security Hub Enterprise reports and notifications will be sent to this email.

isProduction.description:
Indicates whether the client is in production.
**Important**: Once this field is set to true along with productionDate, both fields become immutable and cannot be modified. Any attempt to update these fields with different values will result in a 400 error.
When isProduction is true, productionDate must be provided.

productionDate.description:
The date when the client went to production.
**Important**: Once this field is set along with isProduction=true, both fields become immutable and cannot be modified. Any attempt to update these fields with different values will result in a 400 error.
This field is required when isProduction is true.

size.description: The Client Image's size in kB

uploadPending.description: The Client-Image's is pending to upload

approveUserId.description: ID of User who approve the Domain

title.description: The title of the Inactive Accounts Report. The title is in the format of "IAR-YYYYMMDD".

authorizedUsersCount.description: The number of authorized users included in the report, regardless of their activity.

inactiveUsersCount.description: The number of users that have been inactive for the last 90+ days.

likelyInactiveUsersCount.description: The number of users that have been inactive for the last 60-90 days.

recentlyActiveUsersCount.description: The number of users that have been inactive for the last 30-60 days.

activeUsersCount.description: The number of users that have been active in the last 30 days.

unknownUsersCount.description: The number of users that we could not categorize the last activity of.

InactiveAccountsReportUser.description: A user in a finding of the Inactive Accounts Report.

authDate.description: The date and time when the session was authenticated

expiresAt.description: The date and time when the session will expire

isCurrentSession.description: Whether the session is the current session

OAuthRedirectUrl.description: The APP is already authorized. User should be redirected to the redirectUrl.

redirectUrl.description: The URL where the user should be redirected.

OAuthAuthorization.description: A user authorization for an App

response_type.description: The type of OAuth grant that should be returned

OAuthAuthorizationCodeFlowTokenRequest.description: A request for the OAuth tokens for the Authorization code flow

grant_type.description: The grant type obtained from the user. Must be 'authorization_code'

code.description: The code received upon user authorization

OAuthRefreshTokenFlowTokenRequest.description: A request for the OAuth tokens for the Refresh token flow

grant_type.description: The grant type obtained from a previous token request. Must be 'refresh_token'

refresh_token.description: The refresh token received in a previous token request.

PublicIdentityProvider.description: The public information of an identity provider

referenceId.description:
A user's reference ID of an external service, for example Active Directory.
This field can be used to recognize a user when using a custom Identity Provider.

*IMPORTANT*: If set, this must be a unique identifier between all your users.

email.description:
The user's email

*IMPORTANT*: The email will be converted into lowercase.

employeeId.description: The user's employee ID in actual client

profileId.description: The user's profile ID in actual client. This refer to the `id` of the **Profile** Entity. See [Profile](/v2/service/id#tag/Profile).

lang.description:
The user's preferred lang.
This field refer to the `fullCode` of the **Language** Entity. See [Language](/v2/service/i18n#tag/Languages).

clientId.description: The user's actual client ID. See [Client](/v2/service/id#tag/Client).

hasAccessToAllLocations.description: Indicates if the user has access to all Locations in actual client, despite of the `locations` property.

locations.description: The Locations IDs that the user has access to. This refer to the `id` of the **Location** Entity. See [Location](/v2/service/commerce#tag/Location).

warehousesIds.description:
The Warehouses IDs that the user has access to. This refer to the `id` of the **Warehouse** Entity. See [Warehouse](/v2/service/wms#tag/Warehouse).

If you set a Location without any of its warehouses, it means that will has access to every warehouse.

effectiveAccessWarehousesIds.description: The Warehouses IDs that User has effective access.

lastPasswordChangeDate.description: The user's last password change date

unlock.description: Set to true to unlock a locked user

locked.description: The unixtime in seconds when the user was locked. Only present when a user is locked.

lockedDate.description: The date when the user was locked. Only present when a user is locked.

lockedExpirationDate.description: The date when the user was lock expires. Only present when a user is locked.

isExternal.description: Whether user is external or not.

isReadOnly.description: Whether user will only be allowed to perform read operations, even if it has write permissions set on their profile.

card.description: User card ID for current client.

password.description: The user's password. If not set, it won't be able to login using email and password.

temporalPassword.description: The user's temporal password. It is used to reset the main password.

UserAuthorizedApp.description: An App authorized by the user

authorizationDate.description: The date when the app was authorized

x-janis-total.description: The total amount of records in the list

delete.description:
Revoke an active API Key.

A revoked API Key will have its status set to `revoked` and will be automatically pruned after 30 days.

get.summary: Get a list of API Keys
get.description: Get a list of all API Keys.

Save the account of the logged User.
All fields in the request are optional, only one field needs to be received.

requestBody.description: The data to save in the Account of a User

currentPassword.description: The account current password. This is required to set a new password.

state.description: The APP state sent when password recovery was required

This endpoint returns the current client tiers for each module.

:information_source: The result may be cached for a while after updating the tiers.

id.description: The user ID (only when an external user is created)

clients.description: The clients associated with this organization

requestBody.description: The User Data to recover the password

state.description: The APP current state so it can be reclaimed upon password change

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The name of the entity that owns the settings to be updated

Reject a pending authorized user request
Revoke an approved authorized user request. Can only be done before expiration.

get.description:
Get an inactive accounts report by ID.

🛡️ **Security Hub Enterprise** is required to get Inactive Accounts Protection.

get.summary: Get a list of Inactive Accounts Reports
get.description:
Get a list of inactive accounts reports.

🛡️ **Security Hub Enterprise** is required to get Inactive Accounts Protection.

get.summary: Get the users of an Inactive Accounts Report
get.description:
Get the users of an inactive accounts report.

🛡️ **Security Hub Enterprise** is required to get Inactive Accounts Protection.

get.summary: Get a list of my sessions

post.summary: Creates an API Key for a user
post.description:
**Important**: For creating API Keys for other users than current user, the permission `id:user-api-key:write` is mandatory.
In case of not having the permission, a user can only create their **own API Key**.

get.summary: Get a list of user's API Keys
get.description:
**Important**: To read API Keys for other users than current user, the permission `id:user-api-key:read` is mandatory.
In case of not having the permission, a user can only read their **own API Keys**.

delete.summary: Revoke an API Key for a user
delete.description:
**Important**: To revoke API Keys for other users than current user, the permission `id:user-api-key:delete` is mandatory.
In case of not having the permission, a user can only revoke their **own API Keys**.

A revoked API Key will have its status set to `revoked` and will be automatically pruned after 30 days.

post.description:
Generate a card for a User.

> If a card for the same user and client has already been created, unless it has an error status, a new card won't be generated.

post.summary: Creates a Temporal Password for a user
